name: Rahel Shawl
bio:
Rahel Shawl is an Ethiopian architect and the founder and principal architect of RAAS Architects, an architecture and urban design firm based in Addis Ababa. She graduated from Addis Ababa University and has over three decades of experience designing residential, commercial, institutional, and public buildings.
She is internationally recognized for her contribution to the Royal Netherlands Embassy in Addis Ababa, which received the prestigious ,Aga Khan Award for Architecture** in 2007. Her work is known for combining modern design with Ethiopian culture, sustainability, and environmentally responsive architecture.
Throughout her career, Rahel Shawl has designed numerous notable projects, including embassies, hospitals, hotels, schools, and office buildings. She is also a mentor, educator, and advocate for the development of architecture in Ethiopia, inspiring many young architects through her innovative and socially responsible approach to design.
